rationale: The cited paper appears only as part of a grouped citation supporting a general claim that one brand's promotion can raise category attention and benefit competitors. In the citing paper, this functions as background context for discussing possible positive cross-effects in an advertising-awareness application, not as a source of methods, measures, model structure, or findings that are directly used. The citing paper's main contribution and empirical analyses do not build on this specific study beyond that illustrative literature point.
